prompt_md: Your model provider emails that the **pinned model version** powering your production AI assistant will be **retired in 30 days**. You must migrate to the newer version, but the same prompt can behave differently on it and a silent quality drop would hurt customers. Latency budget and cost matter, and you have an offline eval set plus the ability to flag traffic. **Pick the best migration approach and the correct reason.**

decision_choices: Switch all production traffic to the new model version on the deadline day and watch for complaints
Shadow the new version against the offline eval set and a copy of production traffic, compare quality/cost/latency, then canary to a small cohort behind a flag before full rollout
Refuse to migrate and keep calling the deprecated version until it stops working
Remove the AI assistant from the product to avoid the migration entirely
reason_choices: Because shadowing and a flagged canary let you catch a quality, cost, or latency regression on real inputs before users are exposed, and keep a one-flip rollback if the new version misbehaves
Because switching everything at once is the only way to know if the new version works
Because a deprecated model version will keep running indefinitely if you ignore the notice
Because removing a working feature is a reasonable response to a routine version upgrade
